content:
{
  "title": "Cross-Lingual Prompt Alignment via Dynamic Language Pivoting for LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models underperform on multilingual and low-resource language tasks due to suboptimal prompt transfer and limited in-context examples. Existing prompting strategies often fail to capture cross-lingual semantic alignment.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly adopted in global NLP applications, closing the performance gap on low-resource languages is urgent. With limited training data and in-context examples in many languages, robust prompting methods can democratize access to high-quality language technology.",
  "method": "We propose Dynamic Language Pivoting Prompts (DLPP): for a given low-resource target language, the method constructs prompts by first generating semantically aligned exemplars in a high-resource 'pivot' language, then translating and adapting these exemplars into the target language using both LLM-assisted translation and alignment heuristics. The prompt includes dual-language (pivot and target) exemplars to reinforce task structure while grounding model responses. This allows the LLM to leverage high-resource patterns while maintaining fluency and correctness in the target language.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate DLPP on FLORES-101 and XTREME multilingual benchmarks, focusing on text classification and QA tasks in selected low-resource languages (e.g., Hausa, Lao). Baselines include standard in-context prompts (in target language only) and multilingual chain-of-thought prompting. Performance is measured using accuracy and F1 scores. Statistical significance is assessed across at least five languages representing diverse linguistic families."
}
